reasoning: The control CM-7 (2) focuses on preventing unauthorized program execution and ensuring least functionality. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network policies that restrict ingress and egress traffic by default, thereby limiting the exposure of services and preventing unauthorized access. Additionally, Pod Security Policies (or their replacement, Pod Security Standards) can be used to prevent the execution of unauthorized binaries within containers. Admission controllers can enforce policies that deny execution of specific commands or binaries, ensuring that only approved programs are executed within the cluster.
title: Least Functionality | Prevent Program Execution
